result.markdown:
--- title: "Planning and Control I - Trajectory Generation and Computed Torque Control" --- ## Trajectory A **Path** is a purely geometric description of the sequence of robot configurations. It can be denoted by $\theta(s)$ that maps a scalar parameter $s$, assumed to be $0$ at the start and $1$ at the end, to a point in the robot's configuration space $\Theta$, $$ \theta : [0,1] \mapsto \Theta. $$ As $s$ increases from $0$ to $1$, the robot moves along the path. ::: {#exm-robot name="Sweep of \(s\) in Configuration Space (Path of 2D Plannar Robot Arm)"}
Consider a 2D plannar robot arm with two links -- where the first link length is $L_1=1$ and the second link length is $L_2=0.8$ --, with a configuration $\theta \in \Theta = [0, 2\pi]^2$, and a path $$ \theta(s)= \begin{bmatrix} \theta_1(s)\\ \theta_2(s) \end{bmatrix}= \begin{bmatrix} 2 \pi s^2\\ 2 \pi s \end{bmatrix}, \quad s\in[0,1]. $$ This path can be visualized as follows. ::: {#a32c16d0 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Show Python code"} import numpy as np import plotly.graph_objects as go from plotly.subplots import make_subplots # Parameter on the line segment [0, 1] s_grid = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, 101) # Arbitrary curve theta(s) = [theta1(s), theta2(s)]^T theta1 = 2 * np.pi * s_grid**2 theta2 = 2 * np.pi * s_grid # 2-link planar arm lengths L1, L2 = 1.0, 0.8 def arm_points(t1, t2): x0, y0 = 0.0, 0.0 x1, y1 = L1 * np.cos(t1), L1 * np.sin(t1) x2 = x1 + L2 * np.cos(t1 + t2) y2 = y1 + L2 * np.sin(t1 + t2) return [x0, x1, x2], [y0, y1, y2] # Precompute end-effector trajectory x1_all = L1 * np.cos(theta1) y1_all = L1 * np.sin(theta1) x2_all = x1_all + L2 * np.cos(theta1 + theta2) y2_all = y1_all + L2 * np.sin(theta1 + theta2) # Initial state t10, t20 = theta1[0], theta2[0] x_arm0, y_arm0 = arm_points(t10, t20) fig = make_subplots( rows=1, cols=3, subplot_titles=( "Line segment s in [0, 1]", "Curve θ(s) in Θ", "2D planar arm", ), horizontal_spacing=0.10, ) # (Left) line segment and moving marker fig.add_trace( go.Scatter(x=[0, 1], y=[0, 0], mode="lines", line=dict(color="black", width=3), showlegend=False), row=1, col=1, ) fig.add_trace( go.Scatter(x=[s_grid[0]], y=[0], mode="markers", marker=dict(color="crimson", size=11), showlegend=False), row=1, col=1, ) # (Middle) curve in Theta-space and moving point fig.add_trace( go.Scatter(x=theta1, y=theta2, mode="lines", line=dict(color="gray", width=2), showlegend=False), row=1, col=2, ) fig.add_trace( go.Scatter(x=[theta1[0]], y=[theta2[0]], mode="markers", marker=dict(color="crimson", size=11), showlegend=False), row=1, col=2, ) # (Right) end-effector path and moving arm fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=x2_all, y=y2_all, mode="lines", line=dict(color="gray", width=2, dash="dash"), name="end-effector path", showlegend=False, ), row=1, col=3, ) fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=x_arm0, y=y_arm0, mode="lines+markers", line=dict(color="#1f77b4", width=4), marker=dict(color=["black", "#1f77b4", "crimson"], size=[8, 9, 10]), showlegend=False, ), row=1, col=3, ) # Frames for slider animation frames = [] for i, sv in enumerate(s_grid): t1, t2 = theta1[i], theta2[i] x_arm, y_arm = arm_points(t1, t2) frames.append( go.Frame( name=f"{sv:.2f}", data=[ go.Scatter(x=[sv], y=[0]), # left moving marker (trace index 1) go.Scatter(x=[t1], y=[t2]), # middle moving marker (trace index 3) go.Scatter(x=x_arm, y=y_arm), # right arm (trace index 5) ], traces=[1, 3, 5], ) ) fig.frames = frames # Slider UI steps = [ dict( method="animate", args=[ [f"{sv:.2f}"], dict(mode="immediate", frame=dict(duration=0, redraw=True), transition=dict(duration=0)), ], label=f"{sv:.2f}", ) for sv in s_grid ] fig.update_layout( sliders=[ dict( active=0, steps=steps, x=0.12, y=-0.12, len=0.78, currentvalue=dict(prefix="s = "), ) ], margin=dict(l=30, r=30, t=60, b=110), ) # Axes formatting fig.update_xaxes(range=[-0.05, 1.05], title_text="s", row=1, col=1, zeroline=False) fig.update_yaxes(range=[-0.2, 0.2], showticklabels=False, row=1, col=1, zeroline=False, scaleratio=1) fig.update_xaxes(title_text="θ₁", row=1, col=2) fig.update_yaxes(title_text="θ₂", row=1, col=2, scaleanchor="x2", scaleratio=1) reach = L1 + L2 fig.update_xaxes(range=[-reach - 0.2, reach + 0.2], row=1, col=3) fig.update_yaxes(range=[-reach - 0.2, reach + 0.2], row=1, col=3, scaleanchor="x3", scaleratio=1) fig.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: ### Point-to-Point Trajectories #### Straight-Line Path
